author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

D3b (in `sub4-contractdrift.sh`, added 2026-06-26 followup to c67f162) reproduced the push-rejected envelope via the `git clone + jj git init --colocate + jjf pull` fallback recipe â€” closing the harness gap where the original D3's `cp -R` left both clones sharing jj's op-log so no divergence occurred. Full snapshot in `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/.scratch/d3b/evidence/d3b-hint-snapshot.txt`.

D3b's substring probe confirmed substring-matchable phrases in the `message` field:

- `"retry"`
- `"rejected"`
- `"fetch first"` (raw libgit2 marker â€” version-dependent)
- `` "run `jjf pull" `` (the appended jjforge advisory)

This matches the contract-drift call-out in this ticket's body and validates the proposed fix (move `hint` into `details.hint` to mirror the `concurrent_write` shape; structured `details.refs_rejected` to obsolete the raw refspec leak).

## Recommendation

No new ticket. This comment is the cross-link from the 2026-06-26 followup to the existing finding. The D3b harness is the durable repro for whoever picks this up.

## Confidence

high. Reproduced from a clean scratch repo and snapshot archived; behavior matches the ticket body verbatim.

## Open follow-ups

- none.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Reshaped the `push_rejected` envelope (commit `85b72402`). The wire `kind` stays `push_rejected`; only the message/details shape changed.

New envelope (`details`):

```json
{
  "remote": "origin",
  "hint": "run `jjf pull origin` first, then retry the push",
  "refs_rejected": ["refs/jjf/issues/ccc5d3c"],
  "stderr_raw": "...raw git stderr (multi-line)..."
}
```

`message` is now `"push to origin rejected (non-fast-forward); the remote moved since you last pulled"` â€” short, single-line, deterministic, no raw git stderr.

Files changed:
- `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` â€” `CliError::PushRejected` variant carries `refs_rejected: Vec<String>`; new short `#[error(...)]` Display; `details()` formatter emits the four keys above; `classify_push_error` populates `refs_rejected` via the new `parse_rejected_refs` helper; 8 new `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` unit tests pinning parser robustness and envelope shape.
- `docs/cli-json.md` â€” error-kind table row, per-verb push example, new "Note on `push_rejected`" section.
- `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/sub4-contractdrift.sh` â€” d3b strengthened with a `message`-field check that asserts none of `"fetch first"`, `"hint: Updates were rejected"`, `"refs/jjf/issues/"`, `"git push --help"` appear in `message`.

d3b probe outcome (post-fix): `[d3b] OK: push_rejected message field is free of raw git stderr tokens`. The `details.hint` substring matches (`retry`, `run \`jjf pull`) survive â€” that's the intentional advisory text on its proper surface, not stderr leakage. `"fetch first"` no longer matches anything the probe scans.

Test counts: 520 workspace tests pass (8 new unit tests inside `crates/jjf/src/main.rs::tests`).

## Recommendation

Close. The contract drift is gone: scripts have a structured `details.refs_rejected` for the rejected refs and a `details.hint` for the operator advisory (mirroring `concurrent_write`'s shape). The `message` field is now stable and free of git-version-dependent text. The raw stderr stays available via `details.stderr_raw` for callers who need it for debugging.

## Confidence

high. End-to-end repro via d3b confirms the new envelope shape; unit tests pin both parser behaviour (4 cases) and envelope rendering (4 cases) including the contract that `message` cannot leak raw stderr.

## Open follow-ups

- none.
